comments.text:
## Design Review Rationale — 2026-07-15

This child was deepened around one principle: callers should learn App Settings behavior, not storage mechanics.

The versioned envelope is intentionally backend-only. The frontend needs the current settings, an optional load warning, and a typed update result; it does not need to know which schema version was serialized or how Tauri Store resolves its path. This keeps migrations/defaulting/repair local and prevents every future settings control from branching on persistence details.

A dedicated Settings store is not needless duplication with Workspace Catalog. The two modules differ in ownership and lifecycle: Workspace Catalog is tied to Current Workspace restoration and recovery; App Settings must load with no Workspace, survive every Workspace transition unchanged, and be independently reset/tested. Separate `BEADSMITH_SETTINGS_STORE_PATH` isolation also prevents a settings E2E scenario from accidentally coupling to Workspace fixtures.

The load interface returns usable defaults plus a typed warning rather than failing the whole app. Missing state is normal first-run behavior; malformed/unsupported/read-failed state is not. The latter must remain visible and repairable without blocking Issue browsing.

The store is not rewritten on read failure because an unsupported version may come from a newer app. Only a deliberate user update/Reset is allowed to replace it. Save success is the commit point; failed save must not advance backend confirmed state.

A complete `AppSettings` update keeps one document transaction and makes future prose/mono font additions coherent. Do not expose arbitrary key/value writes or a frontend store adapter. If the merged runtime offers a shared container, use it only as ownership plumbing; do not make Settings depend on Current Workspace initialization.

Cross-epic sequencing was added because the active Workspace integration changes the same `rpc.rs`, runtime initialization, generated bindings, and E2E infrastructure. Waiting for `bsm-kia.5` reduces rework without blocking the independent Markdown renderer task.

title: Persist versioned app settings through TauRPC
